questions.question: Why did Rakesh live with his grandfather?
questions.answer: Because there was no school in his parents’ village.

questions.question: Where was Rakesh’s grandfather’s house located?
questions.answer: In the Himalayan foothills near Mussoorie.

questions.question: Why did Rakesh plant the cherry seed?
questions.answer: He wanted to see if it would grow into a tree.

questions.question: How did Rakesh protect the young plant?
questions.answer: By placing pebbles around it.

questions.question: Why did Rakesh feel sad when he saw the plant after the goat visited?
questions.answer: Because the goat had eaten all its leaves.

questions.question: What showed that the cherry tree was strong?
questions.answer: It grew again after being damaged.

questions.question: What helped the cherry tree grow quickly?
questions.answer: The monsoon rains.

questions.question: Why was the first blossom important to Rakesh?
questions.answer: It showed that the tree had grown successfully.

questions.question: Why did birds visit the cherry tree?
questions.answer: Because it produced cherries for them to eat.

questions.question: What did Rakesh learn from watching the tree grow?
questions.answer: He learned patience.

questions.question: Why did Rakesh feel proud of the tree?
questions.answer: Because he had planted and cared for it.

questions.question: What did the cherry blossoms look like?
questions.answer: They were pale pink and beautiful.

questions.question: How did Rakesh and his grandfather enjoy the tree?
questions.answer: They admired its blossoms together.

questions.question: Why were the cherries not enjoyable for Rakesh?
questions.answer: Because they tasted sour.

questions.question: How does the story show the passage of time?
questions.answer: Through the growth of the cherry tree over several years.

questions.question: Why is the cherry tree special to Rakesh?
questions.answer: Because he planted it himself.

questions.question: What does the cherry tree represent?
questions.answer: Patience and perseverance.

questions.question: Why does the author describe insects and birds in the story?
questions.answer: To show the tree becoming part of nature.

questions.question: What role did grandfather play in Rakesh’s life?
questions.answer: He guided and supported him.

questions.question: Why is the monsoon season important in the story?
questions.answer: It helped the tree grow stronger.

questions.question: How does the story encourage caring for plants?
questions.answer: By showing how Rakesh nurtured the tree.

questions.question: What did Rakesh wonder while looking at the tree?
questions.answer: He wondered if this is what it feels like to be God.

questions.question: What feeling does Rakesh experience while watching the tree grow?
questions.answer: A sense of pride and wonder.

questions.question: How does the story show that growth takes time?
questions.answer: The tree grows slowly over several years.

questions.question: What message does the story convey about perseverance?
questions.answer: Perseverance helps overcome difficulties.
